body.text: The Tetrahedron Protocol establishes the mathematical foundation for P31 Labs' research, proving that the minimum replacement structure for centralized systems is a tetrahedral (K₄) mesh topology.

This paper introduces the geometric proof that centralized systems are geometrically guaranteed to fail, establishing the tetrahedron as the minimum structure that satisfies Maxwell's rigidity condition.
Centralized systems are geometrically guaranteed to fail
Minimum replacement structure is tetrahedral (K₄) mesh
Validated across quantum physics, structural engineering, electrostatics, and graph theory
This protocol underpins the entire P31 Labs ecosystem, validating the Delta mesh topology as the optimal architecture for both biological and digital cognitive systems.
